title: Workflows
subtitle: Author and publish workflow definitions for your tenant

archiveDialog.title: Archive this workflow?
archiveDialog.body: “{{name}}” has no active runs. An archived workflow can no longer start new runs; you can still duplicate it.
archiveDialog.bodyBlocked: “{{name}}” has {{count}} active runs. It cannot be archived until they finish — let the runs drain, then try again.
archiveDialog.blockedAlert: Couldn't archive — {{count}} runs are still active. Let them finish and try again.
archiveDialog.errorAlert: Couldn't archive the workflow. Please try again.

empty: No workflows found. Create one to get started.

noAccess.title: You don't have access to Workflows
noAccess.body: Designing workflows requires the designWorkflow permission. Ask a workspace admin if you need access.

ai.title: AI workflow assistant
ai.intro: Describe the process you want to design — I'll draft a graph on the canvas for you to review.
ai.describePlaceholder: e.g. A junior submits a document, a senior supervises, the CTO signs off, then it routes to the customer.
ai.modifyPlaceholder: e.g. Add a manager approval before the notification.
ai.reviewCaution: Always review AI drafts before publishing.

error.generic: The assistant couldn't complete that request. Please try again.
error.notEntitled: AI features aren't included in your current plan.
error.notConfigured: No AI provider is configured for this workspace yet. Set one up in Settings → AI.
error.forbidden: You don't have permission to use the AI assistant.

modeler.loadError: Failed to load the workflow designer.

publish.dialogBody: Publishing validates and deploys the current diagram as a new immutable revision. The deployed version cannot be changed afterward — choose Edit later to start the next revision.
publish.error: Publish failed — fix the validation errors and try again.

properties.title: Properties
properties.runsPlaceholder: Run history appears here once this definition is published (E1.2).
properties.empty: Select an element to edit its properties.
properties.name: Name

validation.invalidOption: Choose one of the allowed values.

checkpoint.heading: AI checkpoint
checkpoint.hint: The AI checks the work against your criteria, then a gateway routes the verdict.

criteria.label: Criteria
criteria.hint: What the AI must check before the work moves on.
criteria.required: Add at least one criterion with an id and a description.

minConfidence.label: Minimum confidence
minConfidence.hint: Below this, the verdict is sent to a human (0–1).

autonomy.label: Autonomy
autonomy.hint: How a confident pass is routed. v1 auto-forwards; everything else goes to a human.

content.label: Content to evaluate
content.hint: Optional — usually mapped from an upstream step.

instructions.label: Extra instructions
instructions.hint: Optional guidance appended to the checkpoint prompt.

validation.title: Validation
validation.runHint: Run validation to check this diagram before publishing.

class.subflow-interface-unsatisfied: Interface port not satisfied by body

title.tooltip: Short label for this approval step.
title.description: Shown on the task card and in notifications.
